macro_context.market_sentiment: Mixed-to-cautious on May 4: Dow futures declined on escalating US-Iran conflict after reports of missile strikes on a US warship near Jask island, driving oil prices higher. Prior session (May 1) saw Nasdaq hit a new record at 25,114 and S&P 500 at 7,230 on Apple earnings beat and broader AI momentum.
macro_context.fed_outlook: Fed held rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29 (8-4 dissent split). Inflation remains elevated partly due to Middle East energy prices. Powell stepping down as chair but staying on the board; rate cuts remain on the table but consensus is on hold amid macro uncertainty.
macro_context.geopolitical: US-Iran conflict is the dominant risk: Strait of Hormuz closure threat, oil price spike, and reports of missile strikes on US warships on May 4. Ongoing US tariff uncertainty and Chinese overcapacity pressure in semiconductors (mature node chips) add secondary headwinds.
key_events: US-Iran military escalation — oil price surge and risk-off pressure (May 4)
Palantir Q1 earnings due after close May 4
NVDA earnings scheduled ~May 27, 2026 after market close
Federal Reserve next meeting (no date confirmed yet post-April 29 hold)
Middle East energy crisis watch: Strait of Hormuz closure risk

ticker_news.headline: Jensen Huang announces Ising, a new quantum AI model, expanding NVIDIA's AI product suite
NVIDIA commits up to $500 billion in US manufacturing as part of $9T corporate commitment wave
NVIDIA and Amazon named big tech winners of Pentagon AI contracts
NVDA stock falls 4 consecutive days; intraday range $197.12-$203.00 on May 3
Nvidia's $20 trillion AI thesis intact but near-term allocation concerns raised by Seeking Alpha contributor

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: No new upgrades or downgrades in May 2026. Last upgrade was HSBC raising PT to $320 on October 15, 2025. Most recent rating action was a reiteration with $250 PT on March 31, 2026. 57 of 60 covering firms rate Buy or Strong Buy; 2 Holds, 1 Sell. Current price ~$208 implies 29-36% upside to consensus ($268-$275 range). NVDA trades ~23% below average analyst price target as of May 1, 2026.

web_context.competitor_activity: AMD is preparing to launch Instinct MI450 GPUs (CDNA5 architecture, built on TSMC 2nm) and its Helios rack-scale platform, targeting yotta-scale AI infrastructure — the most credible near-term competitive challenge. Intel's Xeon/Gaudi accelerators are gaining in AI inference workloads but remain far behind in training. The biggest emerging threat identified by analysts is custom silicon from hyperscalers: Anthropic committed to purchasing up to 1 million Google TPUs, signaling that cloud giants may reduce reliance on NVDA hardware over time. NVDA still controls ~81% of the AI chip market as of early 2026.
web_context.earnings_info: Next earnings date: approximately May 27, 2026 after market close (confirmed by most sources; some cite May 20, 2026 — date range is May 20-27). No specific EPS or revenue estimate figures were found in search results, but NVDA Q4 FY2026 reported revenue was $68.13B (+73.2% YoY), with Data Center revenue at $62.31B. Earnings are within ~3 weeks, creating a potential catalyst window.
